title: FINANCE-DEPTH
scope: Close the real gaps in an otherwise-mature finance/accounting surface. Scout findings: ALREADY BUILT — accounting periods (AccountingPeriod + close/reopen + EnforcesAccountingPeriodLock trait on Invoice/Payment/Expense), double-entry accounting export (AccountingExportService IIF + Sage CSV + ChartOfAccount mapping + diagnostics), late-fee automation (LateFeePolicy 3-tier + LateFeeService + invoices:apply-late-fees daily cron + waiver), arrears aging buckets (0-30/31-60/61-90/90+ in FinanceStatsService + DashboardService), tenant statement (StatementService + wallet integration), gateway reconciliation (PaymentReconciliationService Paystack live/Stripe stub + ReconciliationReport + daily cron). REAL GAPS: (1) DEPOSIT SETTLEMENT — the deposit ledger (deposit_transactions/DepositTransaction with received/deduction/partial_refund/full_refund/forfeit/transfer types), move_out_deductions, and DepositRefundRequest all EXIST, but MoveOutController::complete() is memo-only: it does NOT journal move_out_deductions to DepositTransaction, offset arrears, update lease.deposit_status, or create a settlement ledger entry. Deposit-received is never journaled either. (2) BANK RECONCILIATION — the bank-statement import UI (ReconciliationTab) + BankStatementImport + BankReconciliationService (queue + reference/phone/amount matching + manualMatch) + BankReconciliationQueue ALL EXIST, but FinancesController::importBankStatement and processReconciliationQueue are STUBS returning 'coming soon' — the engine is never invoked. (3) PERIOD CLOSE has no readiness guard (close while draft invoices / pending bank queue items exist). (4) Arrears aging is aggregate-only (no per-tenant drill). (5) Late fees are cron-only (no manual bulk-apply, no tenant-facing preview). Money is decimal:2 across finance models (NOT cents) EXCEPT DepositRefundRequest which uses *_cents; DepositTransaction.amount + MoveOut.deposit_held/total_deductions/arrears_balance/refund_amount are decimal. LateFeeService uses the bcmath Money class. Reuse: FinanceExportService, FinancesController tab dispatch, DepositTransaction ledger, BankReconciliationService, AccountingPeriod::isDateLocked. All landlord-scoped. selectRaw static aggregates accepted.

findings.title: DepositSettlementService — settle the deposit at move-out completion
findings.evidence: MoveOutController::complete() updates the MoveOut row + deactivates the lease + marks the unit vacant, but never touches the deposit: no DepositTransaction journaling, no lease.deposit_status update, arrears (MoveOut.arrears_balance) not offset. The DepositTransaction ledger + move_out_deductions exist but stay siloed.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Finance\DepositSettlementService::settle(MoveOut, User): in a DB::transaction — sum move_out_deductions for the move-out, create a DepositTransaction TYPE_DEDUCTION per deduction (or one aggregate) referencing move_out_id; offset MoveOut.arrears_balance; compute refund = max(0, deposit_held - total_deductions - arrears_balance) (reuse MoveOut::calculateRefund); create the final TYPE_PARTIAL_REFUND / TYPE_FULL_REFUND / TYPE_FORFEIT row with balance_after; update Lease.deposit_status (refunded|partial_refund|forfeited) + deposit_refund_amount/deductions/processed_at/processed_by; set MoveOut.settled_at. Idempotent (no double-settle if already settled). Phase81 test: deductions+arrears reduce refund; ledger entries created; lease.deposit_status updated; re-settle is a no-op.

findings.title: Wire MoveOutController::complete() to the settlement service
findings.evidence: complete() is memo-only; settlement must run as part of completing the move-out so the deposit ledger + lease status reflect reality the moment a tenant moves out.
findings.remediation: MoveOutController::complete() calls DepositSettlementService::settle() inside its existing transaction (after lease deactivation), so completion atomically settles the deposit. MoveOutDeduction rows carry their reference into the DepositTransaction (reason/notes). Guard: only settle when status transitions to completed and not already settled. Phase81 test: completing a move-out with deductions creates the ledger + sets lease.deposit_status in one request.

findings.title: Move-out settlement summary on the MoveOut Show
findings.evidence: There is no surfaced breakdown of held − deductions − arrears = refund, nor the resulting deposit ledger entries, on the move-out detail.
findings.remediation: MoveOutController::show passes the settlement breakdown (deposit_held, total_deductions, arrears_balance, refund_amount) + the move-out's DepositTransaction rows; MoveOuts/Show.vue renders a settlement card (read-only once settled). Phase81 surface assertion.

findings.title: Settlement i18n + DepositTransaction.move_out_id populated
findings.evidence: DepositTransaction has a nullable move_out_id column that the manual refund path leaves null; settlement entries should link back to the move-out. Strings need en/sw/ar.
findings.remediation: Settlement DepositTransaction rows set move_out_id; lang finance.deposit_settlement.* (en/sw/ar curated). Phase81 lang parity + assertion.

findings.title: Journal deposit-received + backfill held deposits
findings.evidence: A deposit's ledger has no opening TYPE_RECEIVED entry — the ledger starts empty until a refund/forfeit, so balance_after is meaningless for the held period.
findings.remediation: When a lease records a deposit (lease creation / deposit recorded), create a DepositTransaction TYPE_RECEIVED (balance_after = deposit_amount). A deposits:backfill-received command creates the missing TYPE_RECEIVED row for existing held deposits (idempotent — skip leases that already have one). Phase81 test: received row created once; backfill idempotent.

findings.title: Wire the bank-statement import endpoint to BankStatementImport
findings.evidence: FinancesController::importBankStatement returns a 'coming soon' stub; BankStatementImport (CSV/Excel → BankReconciliationQueue, dedupe, column mapping) is fully built but never called.
findings.remediation: Implement importBankStatement: validate the upload (mimes csv/xlsx/xls, max 5MB) + bank_code + optional column_mapping via a FormRequest; run BankStatementImport for the landlord; return imported/skipped/error counts as a flash + refresh the tab. Phase81 test: a CSV import enqueues rows; duplicates skipped.

findings.title: Wire the process-queue endpoint to BankReconciliationService::processQueue
findings.evidence: FinancesController::processReconciliationQueue is a 'coming soon' stub; BankReconciliationService::processQueue (reference/phone/amount matching → Payment record → mark matched, retry/backoff) exists but is never invoked.
findings.remediation: Implement processReconciliationQueue: call BankReconciliationService::processQueue() for the landlord; return matched/unmatched counts. Phase81 test: a queued row that matches an invoice by reference creates a Payment + marks the queue row matched; a non-matching row goes unmatched.

findings.title: Surface the queue + manual match in the Reconciliation tab
findings.evidence: Once import works the queue must be reviewable — unmatched/pending items + a manual match action (BankReconciliationService::manualMatch exists).
findings.remediation: ReconciliationTab shows BankReconciliationQueue items (pending/unmatched) for the landlord; a manual-match endpoint wires BankReconciliationService::manualMatch(queueItem, invoice) (landlord-scoped). Phase81 test: manualMatch records the payment + matches.

findings.title: Import dedupe + error/skipped surfacing
findings.evidence: Re-importing the same statement must not double-enqueue; import errors (bad rows) must be visible, not swallowed.
findings.remediation: Confirm BankStatementImport dedupes on (landlord_id, transaction_reference) and the endpoint returns skipped + per-row errors; surface them in the tab. Phase81 test: importing the same file twice enqueues zero the second time; malformed rows reported.

findings.title: Period-close readiness guard
findings.evidence: AccountingPeriodController::close firstOrCreates a closed period with no checks — a landlord can lock a month that still has draft invoices or pending bank-reconciliation queue items, freezing inconsistent data.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Finance\PeriodCloseReadinessService::check(landlordId, periodStart): returns blockers — draft invoices dated in the period, pending/unmatched BankReconciliationQueue items in the period, (optionally) unvoided $0 invoices. close() refuses (422) when hard blockers exist unless ?force=1 is passed (logged). Phase81 test: close blocked with a draft invoice in range; allowed when clean; force overrides + audits.

findings.title: Readiness checklist on the periods UI
findings.evidence: The periods page offers a bare close button with no pre-close visibility.
findings.remediation: Periods/Index.vue shows the readiness check for the selected month (counts of each blocker) before close; close disabled (or force-gated) while blockers exist. Phase81 surface assertion.

findings.title: Reopen audit + i18n
findings.evidence: Reopen flips status with no audit trail of who/when/why.
findings.remediation: reopen() records reopened_by/reopened_at (+ optional reason) — add columns or an AuditLog entry; lang finance.period_close.* (en/sw/ar). Phase81 assertion.

findings.title: Per-tenant arrears aging drill-down
findings.evidence: Aging buckets (0-30/31-60/61-90/90+) are aggregate-only; a landlord can't see WHICH tenants sit in the 90+ bucket.
findings.remediation: FinanceFilterService (or a new ArrearsAgingService) returns each arrears row's aging bucket + a per-bucket tenant grouping; ArrearsTab renders a per-tenant aging table (filter by bucket). Landlord-scoped, batched. Phase81 test: a tenant 95 days overdue lands in the 90+ bucket list.

findings.title: Arrears sorted by days-overdue + i18n
findings.evidence: The arrears table sorts by due_date, not by severity (days overdue desc).
findings.remediation: Default-sort arrears by days_overdue desc; lang finance.arrears.aging.* (en/sw/ar). Phase81 assertion.

findings.title: Manual late-fee apply (landlord-triggered, on demand)
findings.evidence: Late fees apply only via the daily cron (invoices:apply-late-fees). A landlord cannot apply now (e.g., after fixing a policy) without waiting a day.
findings.remediation: A landlord endpoint runs LateFeeService for the landlord's eligible overdue invoices on demand (reuse the cron's per-invoice logic; same eligibility gates), returning applied count. Surface a button in the late-fees tab. Phase81 test: an eligible overdue invoice gains a late fee on manual apply; an in-grace invoice does not.

findings.title: Tenant-facing projected late-fee preview
findings.evidence: Tenants get no warning of an impending late fee; only the landlord waiver UI exists.
findings.remediation: LateFeeService::projectFee(Invoice): read-only projected fee if the invoice stays unpaid past grace (no write); surface it on the tenant invoice/finances view as 'projected late fee if unpaid by <date>'. Phase81 test: projection matches the policy for an overdue invoice; zero when in grace / no policy.

findings.title: Phase81FinanceDepthSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog
findings.evidence: Six categories need a single presence/behaviour map.
findings.remediation: Phase81FinanceDepthSurfaceTest: DepositSettlementService + PeriodCloseReadinessService bound; bank import/process-queue endpoints no longer stubs (routes hit the service); deposits:backfill-received + manual-late-fee + reconcile routes registered; lang parity (finance.deposit_settlement/period_close/arrears.aging); nav-audit clean.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/finance.md + closeout + memory
findings.evidence: No finance runbook for the deposit settlement + bank reconciliation + period close flows.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/finance.md (NEW): deposit lifecycle/settlement, bank reconciliation import→queue→match, period close readiness, arrears aging, late-fee manual apply + projection. scripts/phase81-closeout.mjs + memory updates (phase81 plan, forward-path, pending-path, MEMORY.md).
